title: Summarize this job description “Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented Environmental Specialist to join our team! …

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Summarize this job description “Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented Environmental Specialist to join our team! In this role you will get to wo rk on a long-term, large-scale remediation project at the Giant Mine Site.

Parsons is the Construction Manager of the Giant Mine Remediation Project. This is a world class remediation project using innovative methods to deliver a safe environment. The project is expected to last over 10 years and is located just outside the City of Yellowknife, NWT.

What You'll Be Doing:

Field and office duties associated with the environmental program at Giant Mine
Collects samples and maintains documentation in record logs
Inspecting work areas for environmental regulatory compliance daily and weekly
Conducting a monthly regulatory audit of subcontractors and work activities (desktop and field audit)
Communicating corrective actions to subcontractors
Overseeing construction and sampling activities daily
Review of subcontractor environmental submittals
Maintaining environmental information logs
Compiling monthly data and preparation of monthly summary report
Manages subcontractor work related to environmental work packages
What Required Skills You'll Bring:

Bachelor's degree in Environmental Sciences, Geoscience, Engineering or closely related field from an accredited institution
3+ years of related work experience
Must have a valid drivers license and capable of driving light duty vehicles
Working knowledge of Microsoft Office
Strong written and verbal communication skills
Must be a team player
Minimum Clearance Required to Start:

Not Applicable/None
This position is part of our Critical Infrastructure team.
For more than 75 years, our experts have designed and delivered the critical infrastructure that connects and protects communities around the world. We work in diverse, collaborative teams, both within the company and with our partners and customers, to plan, design, build, and modernize infrastructure. We take special pride in projects and solutions that improve communities as well as people’s quality of life by promoting economic growth, enhancing mobility, and increasing sustainability and resiliency. Powered by our people, we provide the imagination necessary to support our customers’ visions—and to help them see new possibilities.
We value our employees and want our employees to take care of their overall wellbeing, which is why we offer best-in-class benefits such as health, vision, dental, employer paid provincial care premiums, Defined Contribution Pension Plan (DCPP), Tax-Free Savings Account (TFSA), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), life insurance, paid time off, sick leave, all province observed holidays off, and gym membership discounts to fit your busy lifestyle!
The position may require a COVID vaccination or an approved accommodation/exemption for a disability/medical condition or religious belief as required by customer requirements and some cases federal, state, provincial or local mandates.

Parsons is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility in the workplace. Diversity is ingrained in who we are, how we do business, and is one of our company’s core values. Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels for minority, female, disabled, protected veteran and LGBTQ+.
We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!”

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Parsons is seeking an Environmental Specialist to join their team for their Giant Mine Remediation Project. The project is expected to last more than 10 years and is located in Yellowknife, NWT. The Environmental Specialist will oversee construction and sampling activities, inspect work areas, maintain documentation, manage subcontractor work, and more. The required skills for the role include a Bachelor's degree in Environmental Sciences, Geoscience, Engineering or related field, 3+ years of related work experience, valid driver's license, strong communication skills, and teamwork ability. Parsons offers best-in-class benefits and is an equal opportunity employer committed to diversity, equity, inclusion, and accessibility in the workplace.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Summarize this job description “Advisian is a leading provider of Environment and Society (E&S) services in Canada. The E&S Service Line is a vibrant and growing team covering: Planning, Monitoring, & Management (including marine, atmospherics, terrestrial, and aquatic sciences); Society, Governance, & Engagement; and Regulatory Approvals, Permits & Compliance. We provide a full range of environmental services with a strong reputation for technical excellence, innovation and quality of work within small project consulting and strategic advisory assignments through to large project engineering and construction management delivery. We pride ourselves on the integration of our E&S specialists with our engineering, construction management and project delivery teams. Our aim is to recruit, develop, and most importantly, retain the people who share our vision and strategy.

Within Canada, our Advisian E&S staff also work with our three Canadian JV Indigenous businesses:

Mikisew Advisian Environmental (MAE): MAE is a limited partnership of Advisian (Worley Group) and the Mikisew Cree First Nation (MCFN) in Alberta, operating with a mandate to provide environmental consulting services (including water, geosciences, and regulatory support) within the Athabasca region.

Nu Nenne Advisian Environmental: Nu Nenne Advisian Environmental is a limited partnership of Advisian (Worley Group) and the Cold Lake First Nation (CFLN) in Alberta, operating with a mandate to provide environmental consulting services (including water, geosciences, and regulatory support) within the Cold Lake region.

Tłegǫ́hłı̨ Reclamation Services (TRS) Advisian: TRS Advisian is a limited partnership of Advisian (Worley Group) and the Norman Wells Land Corporation (NWLC) in the Sahtu region of the Northwest Territories.

We have a challenging opportunity for a full-time Intermediate Socioeconomic Specialist to join our dynamic team in North America West working out of one of our offices in Western Canada (Edmonton, Calgary or Vancouver). We are committed to finding the ideal Candidate and are willing to consider remote locations within Canada that can work in pacific or mountain standard time zones. Our ideal candidate will have a strong technical background in socio-economics obtained through previous consulting experience or an academic setting and familiar with federal/provincial/municipal environmental assessments, permits, licenses and approvals for large projects.


Responsibilities and Key Accountabilities:

The Intermediate Socioeconomic Specialist reports to the Society, Governance, and Engagement Practice Lead within the US & Advisian Americas Environment and Society Service Line. Responsibilities will include but are not limited to the following:

Conducting baseline socio-economic studies, including desktop research, surveys, interviews, etc.

Analyzing and interpretation of socio-economic data

Preparing the assessment of project impacts on the socio-economic environment, including consideration of regional and local economies, community services and infrastructure, land use and recreation, and community well-being and safety.


Preparation of work plans, project deliverables, and budgeting to support proposal preparation and other business development activities

Collaborating with technical and professional staff across the company in completing assignments.

Manages the coordination, organization, and delivery of socio-cultural research programs and related community engagement

Participates in the development of socio-economic mitigation and enhancement measures and management and monitoring plans

Quality assurance – responsible for working within the provisions and guidelines of the Quality Assurance system relevant to the Environment & Society Practice

Responsible for personal safety and the safe working practices to keep project teams safe and understand and follow Worley occupational health and safety procedures.

Travel as required for various field programs and meetings.

Remaining up to date on regulatory changes and advising project staff and clients regarding these changes as the relate to socio-economic requirements


Experience and Qualifications:

Bachelor or master’s degree in Science, Social Sciences, Environmental Sciences, Indigenous Studies, or equivalent from an accredited University, and/or relevant work experience
Minimum 2 years experience with socio-economic assessments obtained through consulting, NGO, or academic settings

Preferred Qualifications:

Proven and progressive experience in business development and/or service delivery in project environments
A knowledge of Canadian Indigenous groups, their locations, economic conditions and other social and cultural factors
Knowledge of project development as it relates to an Engineering, Procurement and Construction company
Strong research and analytical skills
Familiarity with federal and provincial regulatory processes that drive actions to minimize negative socio-economic impacts on communities
Practical experience working with Indigenous communities in Canada with working knowledge of Indigenous cultural issues and communities and protocols
Strong project/task management and organizational skills
Strong interpersonal and communication skills
Ability to build productive relationships with professional colleagues, clients, regulators, and Indigenous communities and leadership
Solid understanding of Microsoft office programs including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Provide and demonstrate personal leadership in supporting a respectful workplace.

Advisian (part of the Worley Group) offers an excellent remuneration & benefits package, a friendly, professional, dynamic and flexible work environment along with the opportunity to develop your career prospects within Canada and overseas.


We strive to be an industry leader in health, safety and environmental performance. Our vision is to achieve zero harm to people and assets, and zero environmental incidents. We select people who share our values, beliefs and commitment to this vision and who demonstrate the expected behaviors, competencies and performance associated with their prospective roles within the company.


We thank all candidates for their interest; however, only those selected for interviews will be contacted. Advisian (part of the Worley Group) is an equal opportunity employer.”

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Advisian is seeking an Intermediate Socioeconomic Specialist to join their Environment and Society (E&S) service line team in North America West. The ideal candidate will have previous consulting experience or an academic background in socio-economics, with knowledge of federal/provincial/municipal environmental assessments, permits, licenses, and approvals for large projects. The responsibilities include conducting baseline socio-economic studies, analyzing and interpreting socio-economic data, preparing assessments of project impacts on the socio-economic environment, managing coordination and organization of socio-cultural research programs, and remaining up to date on regulatory changes. The candidate should have a bachelor or master's degree in Science, Social Sciences, Environmental Sciences, Indigenous Studies, or equivalent and a minimum of 2 years' experience with socio-economic assessments. Advisian offers an excellent remuneration and benefits package, a friendly, dynamic, and flexible work environment, and career development opportunities within Canada and overseas. They are committed to achieving zero harm to people and assets and zero environmental incidents, and they value diversity and equality.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Summarize this “Alexsei is a legal technology startup working towards transforming the legal services industry by building cutting-edge AI-powered solutions. We are on a long term mission to build a truly autonomous lawyer. We are a team of software engineers and lawyers working hard to achieve our long-term mission of making the law more accessible; that is, easier to understand more quickly and affordably.

We are seeking a highly motivated and skilled mid to senior level Data Engineer to join our team. The Data Engineer will be responsible for designing, building, and maintaining the data architecture that supports our company's data pipeline. This includes data ingestion, processing, transformation, storage, and retrieval. The ideal candidate should have a strong background in data processing and cloud native infrastructure. The Data Engineer should also possess excellent problem-solving skills and be able to work effectively in a team environment.

Key Responsibilities:

Design and build cloud native on-demand, scalable and elastic data pipelines in AWS
Processing and transforming large volumes of data, in both batch mode and streaming mode, using AWS infrastructure and external third party tools integrated within AWS.
Develop and maintain data models and schemas for efficient data storage and retrieval
Implement data quality and validation processes to ensure accuracy of data
Build and maintain data warehouses
Optimize data processing and storage for performance and cost efficiency
Participate in the selection and evaluation of new data technologies and tools to improve the data pipeline and architecture
Qualifications:

Bachelor's in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field
3+ years experience in data engineering
Demonstrated proficiency in SQL, ETL processes, and data modeling
3+ years experience with cloud-based platforms such as AWS
3+ years demonstrated experience with AWS Batch Operations, AWS Data Sync, AWS Lambda, AWS RDS, AWS DynamoDB, AWS Elastic Beanstalk, Snowflake, Databricks, AWS CloudFormation, Apache Spark, etc.
2+ years of experience using orchestration tools such as Airflow or alternative cloud native tools.
5+ years experience with Python, design patterns, software development
Legally authorized to work 40 hours/week in Canada
Bonus Points

Excellent problem-solving skills and ability to work in a team environment
Strong communication and interpersonal skills
Built an impressive project, a cloud native pipeline previously? Show us a demo!
Continuous learning initiatives
Culture

We are a dynamic team of engineers bringing together iterative NLP model development, high performance systems engineering and effective software development. Much of our work is highly experimental. We are not perfectionists, we move fast, and are not afraid to be on the learning curve. A willingness to learn and a can do attitude are must-haves to survive and thrive in this team.

As a startup we often have to find a balance between speed, quality and cost of development. This often requires us to think creatively and even invent what doesn’t exist. We are ambitious, we take risks, and we embrace failure in a judgment free but accountable environment.

We work in a trust-based, mission-driven, goal-oriented, fully-remote work environment. We enable each other, but also consciously protect our time to deliver high quality work, to help create a thriving innovation culture.

Compensation and Benefits:

Competitive pay plus stock options (salary $90k-$140k commensurate on experience, stock 0.1%-0.2%)
Laptop and monitor
Training + mentorship opportunities available
Flexible work hours and location. We’re cool with remote work! In-person office workspace can be made available in Toronto (if required)
Health Benefits Plan
Annual Company Retreats IRL (otherwise fully remote)
Additional winter holiday (Dec 25 - Jan 1)
Opportunity for significant career advancement
Work for a fast-growing AI startup and make an impact. We are building something legit and impactful, not just another app
If you have any questions or would like to apply for this position, please contact us. We look forward to hearing from you!

Job Type: Full-time

Salary: $90,000.00-$140,000.00 per year

Benefits:

Work from home
Schedule:

Monday to Friday
Work Location: Remote

Application deadline: 2023-06-14”

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Alexsei, a legal technology startup, is looking for a mid to senior level Data Engineer to join their team. The Data Engineer will design, build, and maintain the data architecture that supports the company's data pipeline, including ingestion, processing, transformation, storage, and retrieval. The ideal candidate should have a strong background in data processing, cloud native infrastructure, and problem-solving skills. Key responsibilities include building cloud-native data pipelines, processing large volumes of data, developing and maintaining data models and schemas, data quality control, building data warehouses, optimizing data processing, and evaluating new data technologies and tools. The minimum qualifications required are a Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, three years of experience in data engineering, proficiency in SQL, ETL processes, and data modeling, and experience with cloud-based platforms such as AWS and orchestration tools like Airflow. Alexsei offers a competitive salary, stock options, training and mentorship opportunities, flexible work hours and location, health benefits plan, annual company retreats, and opportunities for career advancement. As a startup, Alexsei has a culture of innovation, trust-based, mission-driven, and goal-oriented, with a fully remote work environment.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
Write me a cover letter for this job “Material Testing Technician Job Overview

Inline Group has an immediate opportunity available for a highly skilled Materials Testing Technician in our Western Canadian Project locations. In this role, you will have the opportunity to work with an exceptional management team on various Geotechnical Investigations and Testing all within a supportive and collaborative company culture. Come join our team and experience the difference!

Key Accountabilities

Safety

· Actively participate in Inline corporate HSE program

· Actively participate in all client mandated safety programs

· Encourage safe work, safety & quality interventions. Lead by example for both fellow coworkers and all project personnel.

Materials Testing

· You will provide technical services in a field and office environment.

· Preparation of reports and data entry.

· You will conduct on site testing of various materials on construction sites such as compaction testing and testing of concrete and asphalt.

· Maintenance and care of materials testing equipment & vehicles.

· Facilitate and maintain strong positive client relations.

· Actively participate in Inline ISO 9001 Quality Program

Minimum Qualification, Skills & Experience

· Strong knowledge of Nuclear Gauges and the safe handling of them.

· 3-5 years of industrial/civil materials testing experience at intermediate to senior level.

· Valid class 5 drivers’ licence with 5-year clean drivers abstract.

· Strong computer skills (Microsoft Office, Windows based Applications)

· Strong problem-solving skills & ability to work under stressful environments.

· Detail oriented and works with a high degree of accuracy and care.

· Excellent interpersonal and communication skills (both written and verbal)

· Ability to work independently and with minimal supervision.

· CSO, CSTS-2020 Basic Modules, Standard First Aid, H2S Alive Certification

· Ability to travel based on project demands.

· Ability to lift up to 50lbs.

Preferred Qualifications, Skills & Experience

· Geotechnical/Civil Engineering diploma or equivalent.

· Prior CCIL/CSA accreditation is an asset.

· Previous experience in soils, concrete & asphalt testing activities.

· Previous experience in piling.

· Previous experience in Geotechnical Investigations is considered an asset.

· Previous experience with Geotechnical instrumentation is considered an asset.

· PDA testing experience is considered an asset.

· Locally based in a Western Canadian Province.

Fly In, Fly Out (FIFO) Project Highlights

Projects are located in remote areas throughout Canada
Accommodations and meals are provided at zero cost to the employee
Fully paid flights or a flight allowance are provided to the employee to assist with transportation to and from the project site. Flight arrangements will vary depending on the worksite
80% more over time than a traditional 40-hour workweek (10-12 hour workdays)
Scheduled work shifts (IE. 14 days on, 14 days off or 14 days on, 7 days off). Shifts will vary depending on the worksite
Why work with Inline Group Inc?

· High potential for growth – Inline is growing and will have many options for supervisory and leadership positions.

· We invest in our people – we believe in investing in our people to help them grow and become the best version of themselves.

· Your voice will always be heard – we do not believe in top-down management. We value everyone's opinion.

· At Inline Group Inc. we hire people who share our values – Productive and Hardworking, Personable, Professional, Humble and Adaptable.

Job Type: Full-time

Work Location: On the road”

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Dear Hiring Manager,

I am writing to express my interest in the Materials Testing Technician position currently available at Inline Group. I am confident that my skills and experience in industrial/civil materials testing align with the requirements of the job.

With 3 years of experience as a material testing technician, I have well-rounded knowledge of nuclear gauges and the safe handling of them, as well as experience in testing materials such as concrete, asphalt, and other construction-related materials. I possess excellent problem-solving skills, attention to detail, and the ability to work under stressful environments.

As a highly motivated
